output:
I’m locating the implementation for `27-2-derive-position-from-snapshot-state-path:quality` and the surrounding tests first, then I’ll review the changed code against correctness, security, architecture, and AC coverage.I found the implementation in `src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts` and its tests. Next I’m reading the story spec plus the exact diff so the review is grounded in the intended behavior, not just the current code.I’ve got the diff and spec. I’m checking the rest of the visualizer and workflow types now to validate whether the new parser matches the real persisted snapshot shape or only the updated tests.I’m validating this against the actual machine contracts now. After that I’ll run a focused build/test check, because there’s already one suspicious type reference in the new parser and I want concrete failures if they exist.I’ve got enough context to look for concrete mismatches now. The main question is whether the parser handles all valid workflow topologies and all terminal snapshots, not just the one nested-epic shape in the new tests.I found two concrete correctness gaps and one coverage gap. I’m pulling exact line references from the machines and tests now so the review calls out the broken cases precisely.1. [src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts:264](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts#L264) ignores `currentStoryIndex` and `currentStepIndex` entirely and derives position only from `workflowState.tasks_completed`. That violates the story’s parser contract in [_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-2-derive-position-from-snapshot-state-path.md:97](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-2-derive-position-from-snapshot-state-path.md#L97) and breaks valid snapshots the machines can produce. I verified a flat snapshot with `currentStepIndex: 2` still returns `activeStepIndex: 0`, so AC8 is only partially implemented and any nonzero active step in flat mode is rendered incorrectly.

2. [src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts:273](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts#L273) misreports halted/interrupted epic-level steps after all stories are done. In the real epic machine, the active epic step is tracked by `currentStepIndex` ([src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts:150](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts#L150), [src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts:272](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts#L272)), but this parser throws that away. I verified a halted snapshot at `currentStepIndex: 1` comes back as `story_flow` failed and `retro` pending, instead of marking `retro` failed. That breaks AC5/AC6/AC7 for terminal epic-step snapshots.

3. Test coverage does not protect the broken cases above. The `allDone` test only asserts “no active marker” and uses a minimal synthetic snapshot rather than a realistic persisted shape ([src/lib/__tests__/workflow-visualizer.test.ts:538](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-visualizer.test.ts#L538)), so it does not actually check AC4’s “every step has `✓`” requirement. The flat-workflow test also never asserts that `currentStepIndex` is honored ([src/lib/__tests__/workflow-visualizer.test.ts:657](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-visualizer.test.ts#L657)), which is why issue 1 passes green.

Security issues: none found in this change set.

I reran `npm run build` and `npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/workflow-visualizer.test.ts`; both passed locally.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>1) snapshotToPosition ignores currentStoryIndex/currentStepIndex and therefore renders valid flat and nonzero-step snapshots incorrectly. 2) halted/interrupted epic-level snapshots are misreported because epic step selection is inferred from tasks_completed instead of currentStepIndex. 3) tests do not cover the required allDone and flat-workflow behaviors strongly enough to catch those bugs.</issues>
<metrics tests-passed="48" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="3" />
